name: Butterscotch Cream Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare and bake the crust.
2. To make the filling, combine 2 cups milk, sugar, and salt in a nonreactive saucepan; whisk once to mix and bring to a boil over low heat.
3. Place the remaining 1/2 cup milk in a mixing bowl and whisk in cornstarch, then eggs. Return the milk and cornstarch mixture to a boil over low heat.
4. Whisk about one-third of the boiling mixture into the egg mixture, then return the mixture to a boil once more.
5. Continue whisking until the filling thickens and comes to a boil. Allow to boil, whisking constantly, for about 30 seconds.
6. Remove from heat, whisk in butter and vanilla; pour into a nonreactive bowl.
7. Press plastic wrap against the surface of the filling and chill until it is approximately 75 degrees.
8. Spread the cooled filling evenly over the cooled crust.
9. To finish the pie, whip the cream with sugar and vanilla until it holds a firm peak. Use a hand mixer on medium speed or a heavy-duty mixer fitted with the whisk.
10. Spread the whipped cream over the filling, making sure it touches the edges of the crust all around.
Yield: One 9 inch pie
